title: Implement announce-derived network location ingestion
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN-zte2.9 can add a software-only network-location producer boundary, but the real RF/mesh source should be announce or gradient coordinate metadata described in spec/05-routing.md and that C announce/gradient table does not exist yet. What needs to be done: implement decoded mesh announce/gradient coordinate storage, feed validated coordinates into the network-location producer, define privacy/source-name behavior, and validate with simulator/no-hardware tests plus later RF integration where applicable.
notes:
Implemented no-hardware decoded announce coordinate ingestion boundary for gateway network location. Added int24 app_data type 0x01 decoder (signed 24-bit 1e-5 degrees -> e7), bounded announce record table, mutex-protected submit/get/reset, sequence serial arithmetic with freshness timeout reset, uptime-wrap-aware eviction, privacy withdrawal, and fallback to another fresh stored peer when the current published peer omits coords. Added rollback of announce table/published-peer state when app-interface/HAL publish, fallback, or clear side effects fail so the same announce seq can be retried. Added direct-vs-announce network source ownership so a later direct network producer is not cleared by an old announce withdrawal. Added HAL/app-interface scoped NETWORK source clear so privacy withdrawal does not clear local/manual/onboard location sources. Added app-interface test hooks for one-shot location submit and network clear failures under CONFIG_LICHEN_APP_INTERFACE_TEST_HOOKS.

Production verified RF announce RX caller is not present in this slice; created project-LICHEN-r9bb to wire verified LICHEN announce frames into this boundary and chain RF validation. Captured user build-time timestamp GNSS/time epoch idea as project-LICHEN-fox3. Created project-LICHEN-qbgj for the protocol issue that signed int24 at 1e-5 degrees cannot encode full longitude range.

Reviews: initial review rounds found privacy withdrawal, table exhaustion, locking, seq freshness/wrap, fallback, side-effect race, wrap-aware eviction, missing source-scoped clear coverage, stale cross-peer publish, side-effect failure retry, and direct NETWORK ownership issues; fixed all P0/P1/P2 findings. Final independent reviews by Mill the 9th and Ohm the 9th reported no P0/P1/P2 findings.

Validation on EC2 us-east-2a instance i-0a3288920ad0ba530 using EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 in isolated worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-zte2-9-1: git diff --check passed. Command: west twister -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_gateway_adapter -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --inline-logs --outdir twister-out-zte2-9-1. Result: passed 2/2 executed configs, 0 failed, 0 errored, 2 skipped, 134 test cases, no warnings, 30.86s. Size output: native_sim zephyr.elf text=299607 data=7895 bss=76190 dec=383692; native_sim zephyr.exe text=319416 data=8974 bss=77088 dec=405478; qemu_x86 zephyr.elf text=256044 data=6252 bss=155956 dec=418252.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware announce-derived network location ingestion, reviewed, EC2 Twister validated, and follow-up Beads filed for RF wiring/protocol/build-epoch work.
